content:
mkdir intro-to-git
mkdir intro-to-git/topics
mkdir intro-to-git/topics/01-what-is-git
mkdir intro-to-git/topics/02-making-commits
label: Create the folder structure

body: Start simple. Create your folder structure and `course.json` first, then add one topic at a time. Get each topic loading in the app before moving on to the next. Iterating in small steps is much easier than debugging a large course all at once.

content:
{
  "id": "intro-to-git",
  "title": "Intro to Git",
  "description": "Learn the basics of Git version control.",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "author": "Your Name",
  "tags": ["git", "version-control", "beginner"],
  "topicOrder": [
    "01-what-is-git",
    "02-making-commits"
  ]
}
label: intro-to-git/course.json

body: The `id` field must be unique across all courses you load. Use kebab-case and keep it short. The `topicOrder` entries must match the folder names inside `topics/` exactly — including the numeric prefix.

label: intro-to-git/topics/01-what-is-git/content.json
intro-to-git/topics/02-making-commits/content.json

content:
intro-to-git/
├── course.json
└── topics/
    ├── 01-what-is-git/
    │   ├── content.json
    │   └── intro.md
    └── 02-making-commits/
        └── content.json
label: Final folder structure

question: Which of the following is a valid folder name for a third topic about branching in git?
explanation: The folder name needs a numeric prefix for ordering (such as 03) followed by a hyphen and a kebab-case description. Examples like 03-branching, 03-git-branching, or 03-branches-and-merging would all be valid. The numeric prefix determines display order, and the rest becomes the display title.
